page_title: Termination law and at-will exceptions — Nebraska

sources.title: Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-1230 — Nebraska final-pay statute
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-1101 et seq. — Nebraska Fair Employment Practice Act (NEFEPA)
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-645 — Workers' compensation retaliation protection
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 81-2701 et seq. — State Employees Whistleblower Protection Act
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-1228 et seq. — Nebraska Wage Payment and Collection Act
Federal WARN Act — U.S. Department of Labor

case_law.citation: Schriner v. Meginnis Ford Co., 421 N.W.2d 755 (Neb. 1988)
case_law.significance: Nebraska Supreme Court establishing the narrow public-policy wrongful-discharge exception. Four categories: refusing to commit a crime, performing a public obligation, exercising a statutory right, reporting violations under specific whistleblower statutes.

nebraska_facts_anchored: Schriner v. Meginnis Ford Co. narrow public-policy exception (not Iowa open case-law map)
NEFEPA 15-employee threshold (matches Title VII, not Iowa/Kansas 4-employee floor)
NEFEPA marital-status protected class (not in Title VII)
Neb. Rev. Stat. § 48-645 workers comp retaliation statute (independent of Schriner)
No penalty multiplier for unpaid final wages (unlike Iowa § 91A.8 double wages)
